title: First standardâ†”standard association batch for expert review (crosswalk layer pilot)
context: OER are abundant and free but are not indexed against the curriculum standards that govern what teachers must teach, so teachers cannot find the free resource aligned to a given standard. This project builds an open crosswalk with two record types: AlignmentRecord (OERâ†”standard) and StandardAssociation (standardâ†”standard). Two hard gates never skip: the dual licence/terms gate (gate-002) and expert review of every record, with a blind second-reviewer precision audit holding precision at >= 95%. PLAN.md v0.2 adds an M2 deliverable: stand up the first standardâ†”standard association batch for expert review alongside the scaled OER alignments. Every StandardAssociation is an expert-reviewed proposal (notEndorsement:true) under the same dual gate and review model as AlignmentRecords â€” never an authoritative equivalence claim. The association layer is the project's hardest-to-copy differentiator: Academic Benchmarks sells these correlations commercially; this dataset opens them.
objective: Produce the first batch of standardâ†”standard StandardAssociation candidates for one framework pair, pass them through expert review under the dual gate, and publish the accepted associations as a CC-BY dataset.
acceptanceCriteria: Framework pair chosen and passed gate-002 (framework terms confirmed for both frameworks, both permitsReference:true); pair selection documented with rationale (e.g. CCSSâ†”a state standard or Australian NCâ†”England NC for one subject/grade band).

Each StandardAssociation candidate carries: from/to standard references (frameworkId, statementId, caseGuid), associationType (exactMatch | isEquivalentTo | isRelatedTo | isPartOf | supersedes), strength, coverage, evidence, aiConfidence, notEndorsement:true.

All candidate associations pass through expert review by a qualified reviewer with expertise in both frameworks; review.status:accepted is the ship signal â€” no AI-proposed association ships without expert sign-off.
Every caseGuid resolves against the official CASE Network source; all records validate against the StandardAssociation schema (depends on stdassoc-032).
Published as a CC-BY-4.0 open dataset with a gate artifact and a provenance record; notEndorsement:true is present in every exported record; commit is DCO signed-off.
resources: PLAN.md (v0.2 â€” StandardAssociation record type, M2 standardâ†”standard batch, hard guardrails, competitive differentiation)
